name: WellFit Health Claims Guard
purpose: Machine-readable guardrail for WellFit health, fitness, buddy, mission, and marketing wording so beta-facing language stays motivational, non-medical, and review-first.
scope: marketing, onboarding, dashboard, mobile, and challenge wording before release

forbiddenClaims.label: medizinische Heilversprechen
forbiddenClaims.rule: Do not claim that WellFit, movement missions, the buddy, AR experiences, rewards, or learning modules cure, heal, reverse, eliminate, prevent, or treat medical conditions.

forbiddenClaims.label: Diagnose
forbiddenClaims.rule: Do not diagnose users, infer diseases, label medical status, or present app signals, watch data, camera data, location context, or questionnaire answers as a diagnosis.

forbiddenClaims.label: Therapieersatz
forbiddenClaims.rule: Do not state or imply that WellFit replaces physicians, therapists, prescribed treatment, medication, emergency care, rehabilitation, or professional medical advice.

forbiddenClaims.label: garantierte Gesundheitswirkung
forbiddenClaims.rule: Do not guarantee weight loss, pain relief, lower stress, better sleep, recovery, symptom improvement, disease prevention, or any other health outcome.

forbiddenClaims.label: krankheitsbezogene Aussagen ohne Prüfung
forbiddenClaims.rule: Do not publish disease-, symptom-, recovery-, injury-, mental-health-, or treatment-related claims unless a separate human medical/legal/product review explicitly approves the exact wording and evidence basis.

allowedCautiousLanguage.label: Bewegung kann unterstützen
allowedCautiousLanguage.pattern: Movement can support routines, energy, learning breaks, and a more active day when suitable for the user.

allowedCautiousLanguage.label: Motivation
allowedCautiousLanguage.pattern: WellFit may motivate users with reminders, missions, streaks, playful feedback, and optional social encouragement.

allowedCautiousLanguage.label: allgemeines Wohlbefinden
allowedCautiousLanguage.pattern: Use broad wellbeing language such as general wellbeing, feeling active, balanced routines, confidence, focus, or enjoyment without promising clinical outcomes.

allowedCautiousLanguage.label: keine medizinische Beratung
allowedCautiousLanguage.pattern: State clearly that WellFit does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment and that users should consult qualified professionals for medical questions.

reviewRules: Use cautious, non-guaranteed wording for movement, motivation, and general wellbeing.

Mark medical-adjacent wording as review_required when it mentions disease, symptoms, injury, recovery, therapy, diagnosis, treatment, medication, pain, mental health, biometric interpretation, or guaranteed outcomes.
Keep health, watch, camera, AR, and location data as context or safety signals only; they must not become medical authority or final reward authority.
Prefer beta-safe disclaimers and alternatives over pressure, urgency, fear, or certainty.
Escalate exact copy and evidence requirements before publishing any claim that could be interpreted as medical, disease-related, or therapeutic.
nextSafeMaintenanceTask: Run the health claims guard check when changing health-adjacent copy, then escalate review_required wording before user-facing release.
